##NAMES=Diegogattaite
##RRUFFID=R120156
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_2_CaCu_2_Si_8_O_20_·H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, Kalahari manganese field, Northern Cape Province, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Marcus Origlieri
##DESCRIPTION=Lighter greenish blue massive, associated with darker blue wesselsite and quartz
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R120156
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.98_Mg_0.01_)(Ca_0.98_Cu_0.02_)Cu_2.00_Si_8_O_20.00_·H_2_O ; The mineral is medium gray, associated with wesselsite (white) and quartz (dark gray)  Normalized to 8 Si atoms  water assumed from crystal structure solution
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.2377 b: 15.7441 c: 10.5305 alpha: 90 beta: 125.156 gamma: 90 volume: 1658.83 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
